code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Clean object values - remove quotes from strings
    def clean_value(val: str) -> str:
        if val.startswith('"') and val.endswith('"'):
            return val[1:-1]
        return val

    # Group triples by subject
    subject_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Find the main subject (the one with most triples)
    main_subject = max(subject_triples.keys(), key=lambda s: len(subject_triples[s]))

    # Predicate to text mappings with better sentence structures
    predicate_templates = {
        "author": lambda s, o: f"written by {o}",
        "isbnNumber": lambda s, o: f"with ISBN {o}",
        "followedBy": lambda s, o: f"followed by {o}",
        "precededBy": lambda s, o: f"preceded by {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"in the {o}",
        "mediaType": lambda s, o: f"{o} format",
        "numberOfPages": lambda s, o: f"{o} pages",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"from {o}",
        "publisher": lambda s, o: f"published by {o}",
        "genre": lambda s, o: f"of the {o} genre",
        "literaryGenre": lambda s, o: f"of the {o} genre",
        "releaseDate": lambda s, o: f"released on {o}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"born on {o}",
        "spokenIn": lambda s, o: f"spoken in {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{o} ethnicity",
        "abbreviation": lambda s, o: f"abbreviated as {o}",
        "firstPublicationYear": lambda s, o: f"first published in {o}",
        "issnNumber": lambda s, o: f"with ISSN {o}",
        "codenCode": lambda s, o: f"with CODEN {o}",
        "LCCN_number": lambda s, o: f"with LCCN {o}",
        "eissnNumber": lambda s, o: f"with eISSN {o}",
        "frequency": lambda s, o: f"published {o}",
        "academicDiscipline": lambda s, o: f"covering {o}",
        "editor": lambda s, o: f"edited by {o}",
        "impactFactor": lambda s, o: f"with impact factor {o}",
        "oclcNumber": lambda s, o: f"with OCLC {o}",
        "libraryofCongressClassification": lambda s, o: f"classified as {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda s, o: f"headquartered in {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"led by {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"born in {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"died in {o}",
        "affiliation": lambda s, o: f"affiliated with {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"located in {o}",
        "state": lambda s, o: f"in {o}",
        "influencedBy": lambda s, o: f"influenced by {o}",
        "family": lambda s, o: f"of the {o} family",
        "genus": lambda s, o: f"of the {o} genus",
        "regionServed": lambda s, o: f"serves {o}",
        "founder": lambda s, o: f"founded by {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"capital is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"known as {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"leader title is {o}",
        "nickname": lambda s, o: f"nicknamed {o}",
        "notableWork": lambda s, o: f"known for {o}",
        "almaMater": lambda s, o: f"attended {o}",
        "doctoralAdvisor": lambda s, o: f"advised by {o}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: f"from {o}",
        "residence": lambda s, o: f"resides in {o}",
        "parentCompany": lambda s, o: f"subsidiary of {o}",
    }

    # Build the main sentence for the primary subject
    main_triples = subject_triples[main_subject]

    if not main_triples:
        return main_subject

    # Start with the subject name
    parts = [main_subject]

    # Get predicates for main subject
    for t in main_triples:
        if t.predicate in predicate_templates:
            clean_obj = clean_value(t.object)
            template = predicate_templates[t.predicate]
            parts.append(template(main_subject, clean_obj))
        else:
            parts.append(f"{t.predicate} {clean_value(t.object)}")

    # Join all parts into a fluent sentence
    sentence = " ".join(parts)

    # Ensure proper capitalization and punctuation
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:] + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
